{"id":"smallhold-8tw","title":"GET /oauth/authorize: minimal HTML login form with persona selector","description":"Render a minimal HTML login page. Shows instance name, admin password field, and a dropdown to select which persona this token should authenticate as. On submit (POST /oauth/authorize): verify password with argon2, create authorization code (32 bytes base64url, stored as SHA-256 hex), redirect to redirect_uri with code= and state= params.

**Agent workflow**: Spawn a research subagent to test the authorize flow end-to-end using curl to simulate what Ivory does.

**Test discipline**:
- POST with correct password: authorization code generated, redirect happens with correct params
- POST with wrong password: re-render form with error, rate-limit counter incremented
- POST after 3 wrong passwords in 1 minute: 429 Too Many Requests
- Verify authorization code stored as SHA-256(code), not plaintext
- Verify code expires after 10 minutes
Tests use independent oracle: check DB directly for hashed code, not via token exchange.

**Defensive programming**:
- argon2id verification must use constant-time comparison
- Rate limit: 3 failed logins per minute per IP, in-memory HashMap<IpAddr, (count, window_start)>
- state parameter: round-trip through code to token exchange without modification
- CSRF: form includes a signed nonce (signed with secret_key) to prevent cross-site form submissions
- No .unwrap() outside test code; all errors Result<T, AppError>","acceptance_criteria":"Correct password creates auth code and redirects with code and state; wrong password rate-limited after 3 attempts with 429; code stored as SHA-256 hash not plaintext; code expires after 10 minutes; CSRF nonce validated","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"mark@wolfssl.com","created_at":"2026-04-23T00:30:29Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-04-23T00:30:29Z","metadata":{},"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"","depends_on_id":"smallhold-sbt","type":"relates-to","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:30:32Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","metadata":"{}"}]}
